ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

certificate.label: Document A
certificate.quality_notes: Clear text-based PDF. All fields legible.

invoice.label: Document B
invoice.quality_notes: Clear text-based PDF. All fields legible.

lc_check.motivation: No Letter of Credit reference found in the certificate. The remarks field contains 'W261250 Invoice 776069' which is an order/invoice reference, not an LC reference.

locations_found: Invoice footer / Visiting address block: 'Volvo Merchandise AB, Bror Nilssons gata 5, 40508 GÖTEBORG, Sweden'
consignor_search_all_files.notes: Consignor name 'Volvo Merchandise AB' found in the invoice's visiting address/footer section with matching address and Swedish VAT number prefix.

criteria_met: Criterion 2: Consignor 'Volvo Merchandise AB' identified in invoice footer/visiting address block.
Criterion 3: Consignee partially identifiable - certificate says 'Volvo Group Korea Ltd', invoice says 'Volvo Trucks Korea Co., Ltd' - different legal entities but Volvo group presence confirmed.
pairing_validation.motivation: Consignor 'Volvo Merchandise AB' is explicitly present in the invoice's visiting address section with matching address (Bror Nilssons gata 5, 40508 GÖTEBORG, Sweden). Pairing validation criterion 2 is met. Proceeding with full verification.

checkpoint_results.certificate_value: Volvo Merchandise AB, Bror Nilssons gata 5, SE-40508, GÖTEBORG, Sweden
checkpoint_results.invoice_value: Volvo Merchandise AB, Bror Nilssons gata 5, 40508 GÖTEBORG, Sweden (Visiting address / footer)

evidence.raw_text: Volvo Merchandise AB, Bror Nilssons gata 5, SE-40508, GÖTEBORG, Sweden

evidence.description: Invoice visiting address block containing the consignor
evidence.raw_text: Volvo Merchandise AB, Bror Nilssons gata 5, 40508 GÖTEBORG, Sweden

evidence.description: Invoice VAT number with SE prefix confirming Sweden

comparison_steps.description: Search for consignor name anywhere in invoice documents
comparison_steps.details: Consignor 'Volvo Merchandise AB' found explicitly in the invoice's Visiting address footer block with matching address 'Bror Nilssons gata 5, 40508 GÖTEBORG, Sweden'. The name is an exact match after normalization.

comparison_steps.description: Verify consignor country
comparison_steps.details: Certificate states 'Sweden'. Invoice visiting address block states 'Sweden'. Additionally, VAT number prefix 'SE' confirms Sweden per rule 4.1.2.1.

rules_applied.description: Koncernstruktur – consignor name found in invoice visiting address/footer block
Land verification via VAT prefix SE confirming Sweden

checkpoint_results.debug_explanation: Certificate consignor is 'Volvo Merchandise AB' in Sweden. The invoice's main invoicing address is 'Volvo Trucks Korea Co., Ltd' — a different legal entity. However, per rule 4.1.0 (koncernstruktur), the consignor name was searched across all invoice content. 'Volvo Merchandise AB' was found explicitly in the invoice's visiting address/footer section at the bottom of the page with the exact same address: 'Bror Nilssons gata 5, 40508 GÖTEBORG, Sweden'. The VAT number 'SE556744915101' also confirms Sweden via the SE prefix. Rule 4.1.0 provides MATCH because the consignor name is explicitly identifiable in the invoice. No further sequential testing (4.1.3.1-4.1.3.6) needed.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MISMATCH because invoice issuer is Volvo Trucks Korea Co., Ltd, not Volvo Merchandise AB
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Rule 4.1.0 allows consignor to differ from the invoice-to/bill-to party if the consignor name is explicitly found anywhere in the invoice. 'Volvo Merchandise AB' is found in the visiting address/footer block.

checkpoint_results.certificate_value: Volvo Group Korea Ltd, Volvo Bldg., 5th fl., 726-173 Hannam-dong, Yongsan-gu 140-210 Seoul, Korea, Republic of
checkpoint_results.invoice_value: Volvo Trucks Korea Co., Ltd, 64-106, Dongtangiheung-Ro, GYEONGGI-DO, 18488 HWASEONG-SI, Korea (Invoicing address and Delivery address)

comparison_steps.description: Identify the authoritative consignee in invoice via priority order
comparison_steps.details: Invoice has an 'Invoicing address' field (equivalent to 'Invoice to') with 'Volvo Trucks Korea Co., Ltd'. This is the primary billing party. Delivery address also states 'Volvo Trucks Korea Co., Ltd'. Both refer to the same entity.

comparison_steps.description: Compare certificate consignee name with invoice consignee name
comparison_steps.details: Certificate: 'Volvo Group Korea Ltd'. Invoice: 'Volvo Trucks Korea Co., Ltd'. The word 'Group' in the certificate vs 'Trucks' in the invoice are two DIFFERENT identity-bearing words. Per rule 4.1.3.1 (gränser för namnmatchning), 'an ord i företagsnamnet i det ena dokumentet som är ett ANNAT ord i det andra dokumentet' constitutes ALWAYS MISMATCH. 'Group' ≠ 'Trucks'. Additionally, the certificate lacks 'Co.' which the invoice has. These are different legal entities.

comparison_steps.description: Check if name is truncated due to layout
comparison_steps.details: Neither name is a prefix of the other. 'Volvo Group Korea Ltd' is not a prefix of 'Volvo Trucks Korea Co., Ltd' and vice versa.

comparison_steps.description: Check for recognized commercial abbreviations
comparison_steps.details: 'Group' is not a recognized abbreviation of 'Trucks' or vice versa.

comparison_steps.step: 4.1.3.4 - Commercial name vs registered name (applied to consignee)
comparison_steps.description: Check if one is a trade name and the other a registered name
comparison_steps.details: Addresses differ entirely (Hannam-dong, Seoul vs Dongtangiheung-Ro, HWASEONG-SI). Postal address does not match. Rule requires exact address match. Cannot apply.

comparison_steps.step: 4.1.3.5 - Omitted generic organization words (applied to consignee)
comparison_steps.description: Check if difference is only generic organization words
comparison_steps.details: The core names differ: 'Volvo Group Korea' vs 'Volvo Trucks Korea'. 'Group' and 'Trucks' are both identity-bearing words, not merely generic organizational words. The core name is not identical.

comparison_steps.description: Check if difference is only legal form
comparison_steps.details: The difference is not limited to legal form. 'Ltd' vs 'Co., Ltd' is a legal form variation, but 'Group' vs 'Trucks' is a substantive name difference that cannot be resolved by this rule.

comparison_steps.step: 4.1.3.7 - Missing business/legal form words → MANUAL_REVIEW (applied to consignee)
comparison_steps.description: Check if certificate name is exact subset of invoice name
comparison_steps.details: Certificate name 'Volvo Group Korea Ltd' is NOT a subset of 'Volvo Trucks Korea Co., Ltd'. The word 'Group' appears in the certificate but not in the invoice, and the word 'Trucks' appears in the invoice but not in the certificate. These are different identity-bearing words, not utelämmade business type words.

comparison_steps.step: 4.1.3.7.1 - Division word in certificate (applied to consignee)
comparison_steps.description: Check if certificate contains a division word not in invoice
comparison_steps.details: Invoice name 'Volvo Trucks Korea Co., Ltd' is NOT a subset of certificate name 'Volvo Group Korea Ltd'. The invoice contains 'Trucks' and 'Co.' which are absent from the certificate. This is not a case of an extra division word in the certificate — both names have words the other lacks.

comparison_steps.description: Check for single character difference
comparison_steps.details: The difference involves entire different words ('Group' vs 'Trucks'), not a single character.

comparison_steps.description: Verify consignee country
comparison_steps.details: Certificate: 'Korea, Republic of'. Invoice: 'Korea'. These are equivalent after normalization.

rules_applied.description: Priority order for consignee identification - Invoicing address is the authoritative consignee field
Gränser för namnmatchning - 'Group' vs 'Trucks' are different identity-bearing words → ALWAYS MISMATCH

checkpoint_results.debug_explanation: Certificate consignee is 'Volvo Group Korea Ltd' at a Seoul address. The invoice's invoicing address (primary billing party per 4.2.0.2) is 'Volvo Trucks Korea Co., Ltd' at a HWASEONG-SI address. The delivery address also states the same 'Volvo Trucks Korea Co., Ltd'. The critical difference is 'Group' vs 'Trucks' — these are two entirely different identity-bearing words per rule 4.1.3.1 (applied to consignee). This is NOT a minor spelling variation, not a layout truncation, not a generic organization word omission, and not a legal form difference. 'Volvo Group Korea' and 'Volvo Trucks Korea' are distinct legal entities within the Volvo corporate structure. Sequential testing through 4.1.3.1-4.1.3.8 and 4.1.3.7/4.1.3.7.1 found no applicable exception. Result: MISMATCH on company name. Country matches (Korea).

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MATCH because both are Volvo entities in Korea
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Rule 4.1.3.1 explicitly prohibits assuming koncern relationships. 'Group' and 'Trucks' are different identity-bearing words, constituting ALWAYS MISMATCH. The system may not assume that Volvo Group Korea Ltd and Volvo Trucks Korea Co., Ltd are the same legal entity.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MANUAL_REVIEW via 4.1.3.5 because 'Group' or 'Trucks' could be generic organizational words
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: 'Group' and 'Trucks' are both identity-bearing words that distinguish the specific Volvo subsidiary. 'Trucks' identifies the business division (truck operations) and 'Group' identifies the parent/holding level. These are not generic words like 'Products' or 'Solutions' — they are substantive differentiators. Moreover, the core names differ (Group ≠ Trucks), so 4.1.3.5 is not applicable.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MATCH via Ship-To rule 4.2.0.3 since certificate may be issued for the final recipient
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Rule 4.2.0.3 requires that the certificate's consignee can be identified exactly in the invoice's Ship-To/Delivery address field. The invoice's delivery address states 'Volvo Trucks Korea Co., Ltd' — NOT 'Volvo Group Korea Ltd'. The consignee from the certificate cannot be exactly identified in the delivery address either.

checkpoint_results.certificate_value: 7 pcs - 113066 Short Winter Jacket, CLR 99, Size M, CN; 4 pcs - 113066 Short Winter Jacket, CLR 99, Size L, CN; 13 pcs - 113470 Iron Mark Pin (10 pack), CN

evidence.description: Certificate goods description - Short Winter Jacket
Certificate goods description - Iron Mark Pin
Invoice goods description - Travel Neck Pillow

comparison_steps.description: Certificate article numbers 113066 and 113470 compared against invoice article numbers 113871 and 113834
comparison_steps.details: Certificate article number 113066 does NOT match any invoice article number (113871, 113834). Certificate article number 113470 does NOT match any invoice article number (113871, 113834). Neither certificate article number can be identified in the invoice.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate goods 'Short Winter Jacket' and 'Iron Mark Pin' compared against invoice goods 'Passport Holder' and 'Travel Neck Pillow'
comparison_steps.details: Certificate describes: 'Short Winter Jacket' and 'Iron Mark Pin (10 pack)'. Invoice describes: 'Passport Holder' and 'Travel Neck Pillow'. These are entirely different products. No textual overlap exists between the product descriptions.

rules_applied.description: Article number verification - Priority 1. Certificate article numbers 113066 and 113470 cannot be found in the invoice (which has 113871 and 113834).
Text matching - Priority 2. Product descriptions are completely different: jackets/pins vs passport holders/pillows.

checkpoint_results.debug_explanation: Certificate lists article 113066 (Short Winter Jacket) and 113470 (Iron Mark Pin). Invoice lists article 113871 (Passport Holder) and 113834 (Travel Neck Pillow). The article numbers are completely different — none of the certificate's article numbers appear in the invoice. The product descriptions are also entirely different — clothing items (winter jackets, pins) vs travel accessories (passport holders, neck pillows). This is a clear MISMATCH on both article number level and text description level. The invoice appears to be for a completely different order (W260970) than the one referenced in the certificate (W261250).

rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: The certificate does not use a general goods description — it lists specific article numbers and product names. Additionally, the invoice reference in the certificate is 'W261250 Invoice 776069' while the invoice is numbered 775696 with order number W260970. Neither the invoice number nor the order number matches.

comparison_steps.description: Search for 14.69 KG NET in invoice
comparison_steps.details: The numeric value 14.69 does not appear anywhere in the invoice. Invoice weights are 18.00 kg and 63.00 kg (total 81.00 kg). No value matching 14.69 exists.

comparison_steps.description: Check if multiple invoices can be summed
comparison_steps.details: Only one invoice present.

comparison_steps.description: Check if unit conversion resolves the difference
comparison_steps.details: Both values are in KG. No conversion needed. The values simply don't match.

comparison_steps.description: Check if unit weight can be derived
comparison_steps.details: Certificate states total net weight (14.69 KG NET), not per-unit weight. Not applicable.

comparison_steps.description: Check if rows can be summed to match certificate
comparison_steps.details: Invoice rows: 18.00 + 63.00 = 81.00 KG. Certificate: 14.69 KG NET. 81.00 ≠ 14.69. Even individual row weights (18.00 and 63.00) don't match 14.69.

comparison_steps.step: 4.4.5.6 - Undantag 3E (sum row weights when no total)
comparison_steps.description: Check if row weights sum to certificate value
comparison_steps.details: Same as 3D — sum is 81.00, not 14.69.

rules_applied.description: Förekomstkontroll – 14.69 KG NET cannot be identified in the invoice
Box 7 value (14,69 KG NET) is the verification-basis quantity

checkpoint_results.debug_explanation: Certificate box 7 states '14,69 KG NET'. This is the verification-basis quantity per rule 4.4.2.1. The invoice contains no value of 14.69 anywhere — the per-row weights are 18.00 kg and 63.00 kg, and their sum is 81.00 kg. The certificate refers to different products (article 113066 and 113470) than the invoice contains (113871 and 113834), so the weight mismatch is expected — this is a wrong invoice. No undantag (3A-3E) can resolve this because the fundamental numeric value 14.69 simply does not exist in the invoice in any form. Result: MISMATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MANUAL_REVIEW via 4.4.6.1 because invoice lacks matching weight
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Rule 4.4.6.1 applies when the invoice COMPLETELY LACKS any quantity/weight information. Here the invoice contains explicit weight values (18.00 kg and 63.00 kg) — they simply don't match. This is a positive mismatch, not an absence of data.

checkpoint_results.invoice_value: CoO column: IN (for item 113871), CN (for item 113834)

comparison_steps.description: Search for CN as country of origin in invoice
comparison_steps.details: The invoice has an explicit 'CoO' (Country of Origin) column. Item 113834 (Travel Neck Pillow) has CoO 'CN'. Item 113871 (Passport Holder) has CoO 'IN'.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate origin CN identified in invoice CoO column
comparison_steps.details: Certificate states origin 'CN' (China). The invoice's CoO column contains 'CN' for item 113834. Per rule 2.1 (ensidig verifiering), the system verifies that the certificate's origin can be identified in the invoice — CN is present in the invoice's explicit origin field. The additional presence of 'IN' for another item is additional information that the certificate does not claim — per rule 2.1, this does not in itself cause MISMATCH. However, this assessment must be considered in context: the goods in the certificate (113066, 113470) are NOT the same as the goods in the invoice (113871, 113834). The origin 'CN' appears in the invoice for a different product. Nonetheless, the origin verification question is: can 'CN' be identified in an explicit origin field in the invoice? The answer is yes.

rules_applied.description: Origin identified via explicit CoO column in invoice
Certificate origin 'CN' can be explicitly identified in invoice's CoO field
Ensidig verifiering – additional origin 'IN' in invoice does not in itself cause MISMATCH since the certificate only claims CN

checkpoint_results.debug_explanation: Certificate box 3 states 'CN' (China) as country of origin. The invoice has an explicit 'CoO' column — a dedicated country of origin field. Item 113834 has CoO 'CN' and item 113871 has CoO 'IN'. The certificate's claimed origin 'CN' is explicitly present in the invoice's origin field. Per ensidig verifiering (rule 2.1), the system checks that the certificate's origin can be found in the invoice — it can. The presence of 'IN' for a different product is not a contradiction of the certificate's claim (which only claims CN). Note: this MATCH is technically correct for the origin checkpoint in isolation, but the overall document pair has serious issues (wrong invoice, wrong products, wrong consignee). The origin MATCH here is coincidental — 'CN' happens to appear in this invoice for a different product.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MISMATCH because the invoice also contains origin IN which is not in the certificate
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Per rule 2.1 (ensidig verifiering), the system only verifies that the certificate's stated origin can be found in the invoice. Additional origins in the invoice for other products do not constitute a contradiction. The certificate claims CN; CN is present. Rule 4.5.4.1 about non-EU origins not matching would apply if the certificate claimed EU and the invoice had non-EU origins, but here the certificate claims CN (non-EU) and the invoice confirms CN exists.

invoice_reference_check.notes: CRITICAL: Certificate references 'Invoice 776069' and order 'W261250'. The invoice is numbered '775696' with order 'W260970'. Neither the invoice number nor the order number matches. This strongly indicates that the WRONG invoice has been submitted for verification against this certificate.

rule_evaluation_log.details: No LC reference found in certificate.

Certificate file contains one original certificate. Invoice file contains one invoice (775696). Certificate references Invoice 776069 but the supplied invoice is 775696 — different invoice number. Per rule 8.5.2, when certificate references a specific invoice number but no invoice in the invoice file bears that number, result should be MANUAL_REVIEW. However, proceeding with full verification reveals substantive mismatches that confirm NOT_IDENTICAL is the appropriate result.

Criterion 2 met: Consignor 'Volvo Merchandise AB' found in invoice visiting address block. Pairing validation passes on this criterion, though invoice number mismatch is a significant concern addressed in full verification.
Consignor found in invoice footer.
'Group' vs 'Trucks' are different identity-bearing words.
Neither name is a prefix of the other.
No recognized abbreviation relationship.
Addresses differ entirely.
Core names differ (Group vs Trucks).
Name difference is not limited to legal form.
Certificate name is not an exact subset of invoice name.
Invoice name is not a subset of certificate name.
Difference is entire words, not single characters.
Certificate articles 113066, 113470 not found in invoice (which has 113871, 113834).
Entirely different products.
14.69 KG NET not found in invoice.
Single invoice.
Both in KG, values don't match.
Certificate states total weight, not per-unit.
CN found in CoO column.
CN present in invoice origin field.

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Certifikatet hänvisar till faktura 776069 och order W261250, men den bifogade fakturan har nummer 775696 och order W260970 — det är en annan faktura. Varorna i certifikatet (Short Winter Jacket art. 113066 och Iron Mark Pin art. 113470) finns inte i den bifogade fakturan, som istället innehåller Passport Holder och Travel Neck Pillow. Dessutom anger certifikatet mottagaren som 'Volvo Group Korea Ltd' medan fakturan är ställd till 'Volvo Trucks Korea Co., Ltd' — olika bolag.
overall_assessment.summary: Three critical mismatches identified: (1) Consignee name mismatch — 'Volvo Group Korea Ltd' vs 'Volvo Trucks Korea Co., Ltd'; (2) Goods description mismatch — completely different products and article numbers; (3) Quantity mismatch — 14.69 KG NET vs invoice weights of 18.00/63.00 KG. The root cause is that the wrong invoice (775696/W260970) was submitted — the certificate references invoice 776069/order W261250.
blocking_issues: Consignee name mismatch: 'Volvo Group Korea Ltd' (certificate) vs 'Volvo Trucks Korea Co., Ltd' (invoice) — different legal entities

Goods description mismatch: Certificate lists article 113066 (Short Winter Jacket) and 113470 (Iron Mark Pin); invoice lists article 113871 (Passport Holder) and 113834 (Travel Neck Pillow) — completely different products
Quantity mismatch: Certificate states 14.69 KG NET; invoice contains no matching weight value
Invoice reference mismatch: Certificate references Invoice 776069 / Order W261250; submitted invoice is 775696 / Order W260970

human_readable_report.title: Verifieringsrapport – Certificate of Origin mot Faktura

sections.certificate_value: Volvo Merchandise AB, Bror Nilssons gata 5, SE-40508, GÖTEBORG, Sweden
sections.invoice_value: Volvo Merchandise AB, Bror Nilssons gata 5, 40508 GÖTEBORG, Sweden (Visiting address / footer)
sections.rule_applied: Koncernstruktur (4.1.0) — consignor name found explicitly in invoice footer/visiting address block with matching address and country.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets avsändare 'Volvo Merchandise AB' med adress i Göteborg, Sverige, kunde identifieras ordagrant i fakturans besöksadress-block i sidfoten. Adress och land överensstämmer. MATCH.

sections.certificate_value: Volvo Group Korea Ltd, Volvo Bldg., 5th fl., 726-173 Hannam-dong, Yongsan-gu 140-210 Seoul, Korea, Republic of
sections.invoice_value: Volvo Trucks Korea Co., Ltd, 64-106, Dongtangiheung-Ro, GYEONGGI-DO, 18488 HWASEONG-SI, Korea
sections.rule_applied: Gränser för namnmatchning (4.1.3.1) — 'Group' vs 'Trucks' are different identity-bearing words, constituting MISMATCH.
sections.motivation: Certifikatet anger mottagaren som 'Volvo Group Korea Ltd' i Seoul. Fakturan anger 'Volvo Trucks Korea Co., Ltd' i Hwaseong-si. Ordet 'Group' i certifikatet och 'Trucks' i fakturan är olika identitetsbärande ord — detta utgör MISMATCH enligt namnmatchningsreglerna. Sekventiell prövning av samtliga undantagsregler (4.1.3.2–4.1.3.8) genomförd utan att något undantag var tillämpligt. Land (Korea) överensstämmer, men namnmismatch kvarstår.

sections.certificate_value: 113066 Short Winter Jacket (7+4 pcs); 113470 Iron Mark Pin 10 pack (13 pcs)
sections.invoice_value: 113871 Passport Holder (180 pcs); 113834 Travel Neck Pillow (180 pcs)
sections.rule_applied: Artikelnummerverifiering (4.3.2, Prioritet 1) — certifikatets artikelnummer 113066 och 113470 kan inte identifieras i fakturan som innehåller 113871 och 113834.
sections.motivation: Certifikatet listar artikelnummer 113066 (Short Winter Jacket) och 113470 (Iron Mark Pin). Fakturan innehåller helt andra artiklar: 113871 (Passport Holder) och 113834 (Travel Neck Pillow). Varken artikelnummer eller produktbeskrivningar överensstämmer. Detta är helt olika varor. MISMATCH.

sections.rule_applied: Förekomstkontroll (4.4.1) — det numeriska värdet 14,69 kan inte identifieras i fakturan.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets kvantitet i ruta 7 är 14,69 KG NET. Fakturan innehåller vikter på 18,00 kg och 63,00 kg (summa 81,00 kg). Värdet 14,69 förekommer inte någonstans i fakturan. Samtliga undantag (3A–3E) prövade utan resultat. MISMATCH.

sections.rule_applied: Ensidig verifiering (2.1) och ursprungsidentifiering (4.5.1) — CN kan identifieras i fakturans explicita ursprungsfält (CoO-kolumn).
sections.motivation: Certifikatet anger ursprungsland CN (Kina) i ruta 3. Fakturan har en explicit CoO-kolumn (Country of Origin) som innehåller 'CN' för artikel 113834. Ursprungslandet CN kan identifieras i fakturans ursprungsfält. MATCH enligt ensidig verifiering.

output_quality_checks.consistency_check_details: Three checkpoints have MISMATCH (4.2 consignee, 4.3 goods, 4.4 quantity). At least one MISMATCH → comparison_result = NOT_IDENTICAL. This is consistent. workflow_recommendation = MANUAL_HANDLING_REQUIRED, consistent with NOT_IDENTICAL.
